extraction.one_line_summary: FUGLSANG gained race lead through relentless tempo in final 10km while POELS attacked final 300m for stage win and crucial teammate bonifications.
tactical_patterns: FUGLSANG rode entire final climb in big gear maintaining threshold pace rather than explosive attacks to gain time incrementally.
ASTANA deployed LUTSENKO and WOODS in early breakaway specifically to drain YATES' team before final climb assaults commenced.
YATES lacked acceleration response when finally forced to react inside 2km, revealing fatigue from chasing earlier QUINTANA probes.
POELS timed counterattack for final 300m specifically after teammate bonifications were secured, exploiting FUGLSANG's 7-second deficit focus.

rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: sustained power output in big gear entire final climb created time gaps without traditional attacks
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: unable to respond with acceleration when attacked sub-2km, only grind back slowly indicating depleted anaerobic capacity
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: perfectly timed 300m counterattack after sitting in showed excellent race intelligence and preserved power
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: spent entire day in breakaways explicitly working for FUGLSANG's GC ambitions rather than own result
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: committed to long-range breakaway attack despite crash complications demonstrating aggressive tactical approach
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: followed FUGLSANG's relentless tempo to podium spot showing elite threshold power sustainability
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: attempted acceleration at 1km but lacked finishing power to sustain move for time bonuses
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: attacked early on final climb but quickly sat forward on saddle indicating immediate distress under pressure
